message:
Command `# Check the smoke_write32 task - it uses AHB force which goes to APB
# The issue is ODR is 16-bit. Let me also check how smoke_write32 handles APB wait
# Let's try a direct test to see what happens when we write/read MODER  
# First quick test: does MODER stick?
cd /tmp
cat > test_gpio_q.v << 'EOF'
`timescale 1ns/1ps
// Quick GPIO register test snippet 
initial begin
  // test values
  $display("16bit ODR pattern for 0xAAAAAAAA written to 16b reg: %x", 16'hAAAA);
  $display("Expected readback with 32b zero-pad: %x", 32'h0000AAAA);
  $display("Correct test write: 0x0000AAAA expect 0x0000AAAA");
end
EOF
# Now fix the Python runner to use -D instead of +define+
echo "Fix identified: use -DTEST_SEQ_X instead of +define+TEST_SEQ_X"` executed with exit code 0.

function.name: execute_bash

extras.command:
# Check the smoke_write32 task - it uses AHB force which goes to APB
# The issue is ODR is 16-bit. Let me also check how smoke_write32 handles APB wait
# Let's try a direct test to see what happens when we write/read MODER  
# First quick test: does MODER stick?
cd /tmp
cat > test_gpio_q.v << 'EOF'
`timescale 1ns/1ps
// Quick GPIO register test snippet 
initial begin
  // test values
  $display("16bit ODR pattern for 0xAAAAAAAA written to 16b reg: %x", 16'hAAAA);
  $display("Expected readback with 32b zero-pad: %x", 32'h0000AAAA);
  $display("Correct test write: 0x0000AAAA expect 0x0000AAAA");
end
EOF
# Now fix the Python runner to use -D instead of +define+
echo "Fix identified: use -DTEST_SEQ_X instead of +define+TEST_SEQ_X"
